title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
scenes.narration: The Himalayas, spanning 2,400 kilometers, contain Earth's highest peaks, including the mighty Mount Everest.
scenes.visual: A map highlighting the 2,400 km Himalayan range across South Asia.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. clean flat 3D illustration, minimal, white background, soft shadows, blue accent colors, educational infographic, high quality. Narration context: "The Himalayas, spanning 2,400 kilometers, contain Earth's highest peaks, including the mighty Mount Everest."

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
scenes.narration: They formed millions of years ago when the Indian tectonic plate crashed into the Eurasian plate, uplifting the land.
scenes.visual: Simple 3D animation of Indian and Eurasian plates colliding, pushing mountains upward.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. clean flat 3D illustration, minimal, white background, soft shadows, blue accent colors, educational infographic, high quality. Scene 2: Simple 3D animation of Indian and Eurasian plates colliding, pushing mountains upward.. Narration context: "They formed millions of years ago when the Indian tectonic plate crashed into the Eurasian plate, uplifting the land."

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
scenes.narration: The range is anchored by Nanga Parbat in the west and Namcha Barwa in the far east.
scenes.visual: A map showing Nanga Parbat on the left and Namcha Barwa on the right.
scenes.imagePrompt: No humans. clean flat 3D illustration, minimal, white background, soft shadows, blue accent colors, educational infographic, high quality. Scene 3: A map showing Nanga Parbat on the left and Namcha Barwa on the right.. Narration context: "The range is anchored by Nanga Parbat in the west and Namcha Barwa in the far east."

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
scenes.narration: This massive range spans five nations: Nepal, India, China, Bhutan, and Pakistan, crossing complex borders.
scenes.visual: A color-coded map showing the five nations sharing the Himalayan range.

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
scenes.narration: The glaciers feed major rivers like the Indus, Ganges, and Brahmaputra, vital for millions of people.

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
scenes.narration: While 53 million people live in the mountains, their river basins support over 600 million people.

scenes.imagePrompt: clean flat 3D illustration, minimal, white background, soft shadows, blue accent colors, educational infographic, high quality. Narration context: "While 53 million people live in the mountains, their river basins support over 600 million people."

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
scenes.narration: For Hindus and Buddhists, these mountains are sacred, with peaks like Mount Kailash remaining entirely off-limits to climbers.
scenes.visual: Graphic of Mount Kailash with spiritual icons representing Hinduism and Buddhism.

scenes.title: The Himalayas: Geology, Geography, and Culture
